globalGate.wave: Quarters-CRUD (C1–C5): manage everything from the UI. S1–S5 + Q1–Q4 previously gated.

unitTests.note: 423 passed, 2 skipped, 0 failed. The CRUD wave added 30 passing specs (mcp-writer 10, quarters-crud 3, primitive-files 6, hooks-crud 7, plugin-writer 4). `npm test` (full) additionally surfaces 2 FAILURES in tests/orchestrator-integration.test.ts — those are the live-SDK tests hitting a STRAY live Garrison gateway on :4777 (the user's own running operative, PID 46463) which defeats the test's skip-by-default; environmental, unrelated to Quarters, the process was not killed. Excluding that one env-dependent file, everything is green.

e2eAll.note: 9 passed, 1 skipped against the seeded sandbox. The 5 new quarters-crud specs (mcp add/edit/remove · skills create/edit/delete · scripts command round-trip · hooks editable-vs-read-only + create/edit/delete · plugin uninstall) all green, plus the prior settings/memory/quarters/quarters-logs specs (no regression from the sandbox-seed/blurb/panel changes). The 1 skip is the pre-existing UNCONDITIONAL armory-install.spec test.skip() (legacy S2 path, superseded by the Quarters model).

verifyApp.via: 5 committed e2e CRUD round-trips + 10 vision-checked screenshots + a VERIFIED 11-beat consolidated walkthrough video

designAudit.notes: All new editors (MCP form, file/skill form, hook form, confirm + drawer) render in the existing shell visual language on the .scrim overlay; alarm-red for destructive; no emoji; no new idiom. Honesty pass updated mcps/hooks/plugins blurbs to state the new CRUD.

globalGate.everySliceVideoVerifiedNote: ONE verified consolidated walkthrough (docs/autothing/evidence/quarters-crud-walkthrough.mp4, 11 beats, all vision-verified, run NOT flagged) covers the headline flow of C1 (MCP add+remove), C2 (skill create), C4 (hooks editable-vs-read-only), C5 (plugin uninstall). C3 (scripts) shares C2's FilePrimitiveForm mechanism and is filmed via the skill variant; it has its OWN committed e2e (command create→edit→delete). It is one consolidated video, NOT five per-slice videos — hence the global gate is completed-with-blockers, not passed. Each slice's true correctness gate is its committed re-runnable e2e + unit specs, all green.
blockers: Per-slice walkthrough videos were CONSOLIDATED into one verified video (covers C1/C2/C4/C5 headline flows; C3 shares C2's mechanism + has its own e2e). Not five separate per-slice videos — this is why the wave is completed-with-blockers rather than passed.
Plugin INSTALL from a marketplace is NOT built — needs marketplace resolution + git clone; stays with Claude Code's /plugin (gated on SP6). Only plugin UNINSTALL (remove) ships.
MCP env/headers are written as PLAINTEXT to mcp.json (matches Claude Code's own format; not a regression). Routing MCP secrets through Garrison's AES vault is a deferred enhancement.
File-primitive RENAME is out of scope — editing keeps the name fixed (rename = a move).
Cooperative-ownership caveat (plugins/hooks/mcp/settings): a RUNNING Claude Code may rewrite these files on exit, same as the settings.json surface. The plugin-uninstall confirm warns to restart.

ENVIRONMENTAL (not this wave's work): tests/orchestrator-integration.test.ts fails under `npm test` because a stray live gateway is up on :4777 (the user's own running operative), defeating the test's skip-by-default. Killing that process or running `npx vitest run --exclude '**/orchestrator-integration.test.ts'` makes the suite fully green.
